message:
caam-5db7: Add alias/rename workflow for auto profiles

- Add CopyProfile method to Vault for non-destructive profile copying
- Create caam rename command with --delete-old flag for optional cleanup
- Rename migrates aliases from old to new profile by default
- Add comprehensive tests for CopyProfile in authfile_test.go
- Update README.md with alias and rename command documentation

Example workflow:
  caam rename claude auto-20260121-143022 work   # Copy to friendly name
  caam alias claude work w                        # Add shortcut alias

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"caam-539","title":"Project context TUI integration","description":"## Purpose
Add project context awareness to the TUI.

## Features

### Project Context Display
Show current project association in the status bar or header.
```
┌─ Claude Profiles ────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Project: /home/user/work/client-a → client-a@work.com       │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ● client-a@work.com    🟢 Pro   [PROJECT DEFAULT]           │
│   work@company.com     🟢 Pro                               │
│   personal@gmail.com   🟡 Free                              │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Quick Association
Add keyboard shortcut to associate currently selected profile with CWD.
- Press `p` to set project association for selected profile
- Shows confirmation message

### Visual Indicators
- "PROJECT DEFAULT" badge on associated profile
- Different styling for project-associated profile
- Clear indication when in a project directory vs not

## Technical Implementation

### Model Changes
Add projectAssoc field to Model that stores current directory association.
Load on startup based on os.Getwd().

### New Key Binding
Add projectAssoc key binding to keys.go.

### Badge System
Extend ProfileInfo struct with ProjectDefault bool field.

## Files to Modify
- internal/tui/model.go
- internal/tui/profiles_panel.go
- internal/tui/keys.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Project context shown when in associated directory
- [ ] PROJECT DEFAULT badge on associated profile
- [ ] p key creates association for selected profile
- [ ] Confirmation message after association
- [ ] Graceful handling when not in any project","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:26:06.709440597Z","updated_at":"2025-12-18T00:50:55.975972489Z","closed_at":"2025-12-18T00:50:55.975972489Z","close_reason":"Added project context awareness to TUI, including association shortcut and PROJECT DEFAULT indicator.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-539","depends_on_id":"caam-dmw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:29:45.832079614Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-539","depends_on_id":"caam-ir7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:29:40.726796796Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"caam-539","author":"ubuntu","text":"caam-ir7 and caam-dmw are now closed. Project associations are available via `internal/project` + `caam project ...` commands, and `caam activate <tool>` can auto-pick association when profile omitted. This task should be unblocked now.","created_at":"2025-12-17T23:56:59Z"},{"id":15,"issue_id":"caam-539","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting project-context integration in TUI: load project association for CWD via internal/project store, show context in header/status, add p key to set association for selected profile, and mark associated profile with PROJECT DEFAULT badge.","created_at":"2025-12-18T00:45:09Z"},{"id":16,"issue_id":"caam-539","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented project context in TUI: resolve CWD via internal/project store on startup, render project line in header, add 'p' key to associate selected profile with current directory, and mark associated profile with [PROJECT DEFAULT] badge. Added unit test covering project association badge.","created_at":"2025-12-18T00:50:45Z"}]}
 {"id":"caam-53s","title":"E2E: Error handling and edge cases","description":"End-to-end tests for error handling and edge cases:

## Test Scenarios
1. File system errors:
   - Read-only directories
   - Disk full simulation
   - Permission denied
   - Symlink handling

2. Concurrent access:
   - Multiple caam instances
   - File locking behavior
   - Race condition detection

3. Invalid data handling:
   - Corrupted profile files
   - Invalid JSON in auth files
   - Missing required fields
   - Version mismatch

4. Recovery workflows:
   - Recover from interrupted backup
   - Recover from interrupted restore
   - Clean up partial state

## Requirements
- Test actual error conditions (not mocked)
- Verify error messages are user-friendly
- Verify cleanup on errors
- Log full error context for debugging","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-17T06:53:06.789456821Z","updated_at":"2025-12-17T07:42:28.626348145Z","closed_at":"2025-12-17T07:42:28.626348145Z","close_reason":"Added comprehensive E2E error handling tests: file system errors (read-only, permissions, symlinks), concurrent access (multiple backups, locking), invalid data (corrupted JSON, missing files), recovery workflows, and edge cases. 27 new tests covering error conditions and edge cases.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-53s","depends_on_id":"caam-7a2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T06:55:14.217730072Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-53s","depends_on_id":"caam-aek","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T06:55:46.485430641Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"caam-5ed","title":"Cooldown tracking after limit hits","description":"Track when accounts hit limits and enforce cooldown periods.

## The Problem
If account A hits a limit and user immediately switches to account B, then back to A an hour later, this pattern is suspicious. Real users don't hit limits multiple times per day on the same account.

## Solution
Track limit events and enforce cooldowns:
1. User marks limit hit: `caam limit-hit claude` (or auto-detect in future)
2. System records: profile X hit limit at time T
3. Cooldown calculated: can't reuse until T + cooldown_hours
4. Activation blocked/warned if profile is in cooldown

## Database Schema
```sql
CREATE TABLE limit_events (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  provider TEXT NOT NULL,
  profile_name TEXT NOT NULL,
  hit_at DATETIME NOT NULL,
  cooldown_until DATETIME NOT NULL,
  notes TEXT
);

CREATE INDEX idx_limit_events_provider_profile ON limit_events(provider, profile_name);
CREATE INDEX idx_limit_events_cooldown ON limit_events(cooldown_until);
```

## Commands
```bash
# Mark current profile as hitting limit
caam limit-hit claude
# Output: Recorded limit hit for claude/work-account. Cooldown until 20:32.

# Mark specific profile
caam limit-hit codex backup-account

# Check cooldown status
caam cooldown status
# Output:
# claude/work-account: in cooldown (2h 15m remaining)
# codex/backup-account: ready
# codex/main: in cooldown (45m remaining)

# Clear cooldown manually (for testing)
caam cooldown clear claude work-account
```

## Activation Integration
When user tries to activate a profile in cooldown:
```
$ caam activate claude work-account

Warning: claude/work-account is in cooldown
  Limit hit: 2 hours ago
  Cooldown remaining: 4 hours
  
Use --force to activate anyway, or try one of:
  caam activate claude personal-account
  caam activate claude --next  (auto-select best available)

Proceed anyway? [y/N]
```

## Auto-detection (Future Enhancement)
Could potentially detect limit errors in `caam exec` output:
- Codex: 'You've hit your usage limit'
- Claude: rate limit errors
- Gemini: quota exceeded

This would be a separate enhancement task.

## Files
- internal/db/limit_events.go: DB operations for limit tracking
- internal/db/limit_events_test.go: Tests
- internal/stealth/cooldown.go: Cooldown logic
- cmd/caam/cmd/limit.go: limit-hit and cooldown commands
- cmd/caam/cmd/activate.go: Integration","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-18T02:47:26.364033647Z","updated_at":"2025-12-18T05:11:02.980029392Z","closed_at":"2025-12-18T05:11:02.980029392Z","close_reason":"Cooldown tracking implemented (DB + CLI + activate warning/--force + tests).","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-5ed","depends_on_id":"caam-bgz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T02:47:38.495473582Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":42,"issue_id":"caam-5ed","author":"ubuntu","text":"Revised description - fix dependency and add --all flag:

## Purpose
Track when accounts hit rate limits and enforce cooldown periods before reuse.
This prevents the suspicious pattern of: Account A hits limit -> immediately switch to Account B.

## DEPENDENCY FIX
This feature does NOT depend on caam-108 (switch delay system).

Rationale: Cooldowns and delays are INDEPENDENT features:
- Delays: Wait N seconds before ANY switch (reduces automation fingerprint)
- Cooldowns: Wait N minutes before reusing a SPECIFIC account that hit a limit

You might want cooldowns without delays, or delays without cooldowns.
The only shared dependency is caam-bgz (config schema) since both need config.

## Implementation

### Tracking
When user reports a limit hit:
```bash
caam cooldown set claude/work --minutes=60
```

This stores:
- Provider/profile that hit the limit
- Timestamp of limit hit
- Cooldown duration

### Enforcement
On `caam activate`:
1. Check if target profile has active cooldown
2. If yes: show warning with time remaining
3. User can override with `--force` or wait

### Clearing
```bash
caam cooldown clear claude/work     # Clear specific
caam cooldown clear --all           # Clear ALL cooldowns (added!)
caam cooldown list                  # Show active cooldowns
```

## Config
```yaml
stealth:
  cooldown:
    enabled: false
    default_minutes: 60      # Default cooldown duration
    track_limit_hits: true   # Auto-track when limits detected
```

## Files
- internal/db/cooldown.go: Cooldown storage and queries
- internal/db/cooldown_test.go: Tests
- cmd/caam/cmd/cooldown.go: CLI subcommands (set, clear, list)
- cmd/caam/cmd/activate.go: Add cooldown check before activation

## Success Criteria
- Can manually set cooldowns on profiles
- Activation warns when profile is in cooldown
- Can override with --force
- Can clear individual or ALL cooldowns
","created_at":"2025-12-18T03:06:23Z"},{"id":59,"issue_id":"caam-5ed","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting implementation: add cooldown persistence in internal/db, new caam cooldown (set/clear/list) commands, and integrate warning/--force behavior into activate.","created_at":"2025-12-18T04:55:08Z"},{"id":60,"issue_id":"caam-5ed","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented cooldown tracking: added DB migration v2 with limit_events table + indexes, internal/db cooldown APIs (set/query/list/clear), new CLI command group cooldown (set/clear/list), and activate integration that warns and requires confirmation or --force when stealth.cooldown.enabled is true. Added tests for DB and activate integration.","created_at":"2025-12-18T05:10:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"caam-5fk","title":"Auto-backup original state on first activate","description":"Automatically preserve the user's pre-caam authentication state.

## The Problem
User runs `caam activate claude foo` without backing up first → original auth is overwritten and LOST FOREVER. This is the highest-risk safety gap.

## Solution
On ANY activate command:
1. Check if auth files exist for the tool
2. Check if they match any existing vault profile (using hash comparison)
3. If auth exists AND no match found → this is 'virgin' state
4. Auto-backup to `_original_<tool>` profile
5. Print: 'Backed up original claude auth to _original_claude'
6. NEVER overwrite existing _original profile (it's a one-time safety net)

## Key Design Decisions
- Only happens ONCE per tool (when _original doesn't exist)
- Silent if _original already exists (idempotent)
- Uses protected profile naming (_original prefix)
- Stores metadata: {type: 'system', created_by: 'first-activate', original_paths: [...]}

## Implementation
1. Add Vault.HasOriginalBackup(tool) method
2. Add Vault.BackupOriginal(fileSet) method
3. Modify activate command to call BackupOriginal before restore
4. Add integration test: fresh system → activate → verify _original created

## Files
- internal/authfile/authfile.go: New methods
- cmd/caam/cmd/activate.go: Add auto-backup logic
- internal/authfile/authfile_test.go: Unit tests
- cmd/caam/cmd/activate_test.go: Integration test","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-18T02:44:21.406320231Z","updated_at":"2025-12-18T04:07:00.583111467Z","closed_at":"2025-12-18T04:07:00.583111467Z","close_reason":"Auto-backup original auth on first activate: added Vault.BackupOriginal/HasOriginalBackup and activate pre-restore safety. _original is protected/immutable; meta.json includes type=system, created_by=first-activate, original_paths; tests added.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-5fk","depends_on_id":"caam-nh8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T02:44:32.524309690Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":41,"issue_id":"caam-5fk","author":"ubuntu","text":"Revised description - fix profile naming:

## Implementation

On FIRST-EVER `caam activate` (any provider):
1. Check if `_original` profile exists for this provider
2. If NOT: automatically backup current auth state as `_original`
3. Proceed with normal activation

## Profile Naming

Profile name: `_original` (not `_original_<tool>`)

Rationale: The `_` prefix already marks it as protected/system-managed. Adding the tool name is redundant because:
- Profiles are already namespaced by provider (claude, codex, etc.)
- The vault structure is: `~/.caam/vault/<provider>/<profile_name>/`
- So claude's original is at: `~/.caam/vault/claude/_original/`
- And codex's original is at: `~/.caam/vault/codex/_original/`

No collision possible. Simpler naming = easier to remember and type.

## Protected Profile Behavior

Profiles starting with `_` are protected:
- Cannot be deleted via `caam delete`
- Cannot be overwritten via `caam save`
- Hidden from normal `caam list` (shown with `--all` or `-a`)
- Shown in distinct color/style when displayed

## Files
- internal/vault/vault.go: Add IsProtectedProfile() helper
- internal/vault/vault.go: Modify Save() to check for protected prefix
- cmd/caam/cmd/activate.go: Add first-run backup logic
- cmd/caam/cmd/list.go: Filter protected by default, add --all flag

## Success Criteria
- First activate ALWAYS creates _original backup
- Protected profiles cannot be modified by user
- Clear visual distinction in listings
","created_at":"2025-12-18T03:06:17Z"},{"id":51,"issue_id":"caam-5fk","author":"ubuntu","text":"[ubuntu at 2025-12-18]
FYI caam-nh8 is now closed: `_`-prefixed vault profiles are treated as system profiles (ls shows [system], delete requires --force and uses Vault.DeleteForce). Meta.json now includes {type, created_by}. This should help implement _original_<tool> safely.","created_at":"2025-12-18T03:48:26Z"},{"id":53,"issue_id":"caam-5fk","author":"ubuntu","text":"[ubuntu at 2025-12-18]
Claimed. Implementing first-activate safety net: if `_original` does not exist and current auth files exist but don't match any vault profile, auto-backup current auth to `vault/<tool>/_original/` before restore. Will also ensure system profiles are not overwritten and add integration test (codex) for `_original` creation.","created_at":"2025-12-18T03:52:59Z"},{"id":54,"issue_id":"caam-5fk","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented first-activate safety net: Vault.BackupOriginal() creates protected _original per tool if current auth exists and doesn't match any vault profile; activate now calls this before refresh/restore and prints when created. System profiles are immutable (no overwrite) and meta.json now records type=system, created_by=first-activate for _original, plus original_paths. Added unit tests + activate integration test (codex).","created_at":"2025-12-18T04:06:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"caam-5jv","title":"Test bundle/checksum.go - Checksum operations","description":"# Test Checksum Operations

## File: internal/bundle/checksum.go

## Functions to Test (identify from source)
Expected functions:
- CalculateChecksum
- VerifyChecksum
- HashFile
- CompareChecksums

## Test Cases Required

### CalculateChecksum Tests
1. **TestCalculateChecksum_Success** - Valid file
2. **TestCalculateChecksum_EmptyFile** - Empty file handling
3. **TestCalculateChecksum_LargeFile** - 100MB file
4. **TestCalculateChecksum_NonExistent** - Missing file error
5. **TestCalculateChecksum_Directory** - Directory input error

### VerifyChecksum Tests
1. **TestVerifyChecksum_Match** - Matching checksums
2. **TestVerifyChecksum_Mismatch** - Different checksums
3. **TestVerifyChecksum_ModifiedFile** - File changed after checksum

### Integration Tests
1. **TestChecksum_RoundTrip** - Calculate then verify
2. **TestChecksum_TamperedFile** - Detect modifications

## Implementation Notes
- Use t.TempDir() for test files
- Test with binary and text files
- Use testutil.Harness for logging
- First: Read checksum.go to identify exact functions","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:01:26.104786624Z","updated_at":"2025-12-20T00:18:10.689821869Z","closed_at":"2025-12-20T00:18:10.689821869Z","close_reason":"Consolidated into package-level test bead for bundle/","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-5jv","depends_on_id":"caam-1mq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:13:15.267076438Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -222,6 +222,8 @@ carol@gmail.com
 | `caam delete <tool> <email>` | Remove a saved profile |
 | `caam paths [tool]` | Show auth file locations for each tool |
 | `caam clear <tool>` | Remove auth files (logout state) |
+| `caam alias <tool> <profile> <alias>` | Create a short alias for a profile |
+| `caam rename <tool> <old> <new>` | Copy profile to a new name (non-destructive) |
 | `caam uninstall` | Restore originals from `_original` and remove caam data/config |
 
 **Aliases:** `caam switch` and `caam use` work like `caam activate`
@@ -249,6 +251,14 @@ caam pick claude            # type "work" at the prompt
 caam activate claude work   # alias resolution works here too
 ```
 
+Rename auto-generated profiles to friendly names (non-destructive copy):
+
+```bash
+caam rename claude auto-20260121-143022 work   # Copy profile to "work"
+caam rename claude old-name new-name           # Original preserved by default
+caam rename claude temp main --delete-old -y   # Delete old after copying
+```
+
 SSH-safe fallback (no fzf, no TTY): use direct activation:
 
 ```bash
